Representative_Docs: ["Iran Protests Quelled Since Deadly Crackdown, Residents Say A heavy police presence and deadly crackdowns on protesters appeared to have largely suppressed demonstrations in many cities and towns across Iran, according to several witnesses and a human rights group. For weeks, Iran has been convulsed by protests sparked by a collapsing economy and a currency in free fall, with the demonstrations snowballing into a broader antigovernment movement. Authorities have sought to quell the protests with brutal force and have plunged the country into a communications blackout that has made it difficult to know what’s happening. Name: 1_injury_villa_salah_arsenal
Representative_Docs: ['Doku, Bobb, Stones, Rodri, Kovacic latest: Man City injury list for West Ham clash By Oliver Thomas | 18 Dec 2025 08:05, Last updated: 18 Dec 2025 08:09 Manchester City will be looking to win their seventh game in a row in all competitions when they welcome West Ham United to the Etihad Stadium for their latest Premier League fixture on Saturday afternoon. The Citizens are in high spirits after securing their spot in the semi-finals of the EFL Cup with a 2-0 home victory over Brentford on Wednesday night, Prior to that triumph, Pep Guardiola’s men beat Crystal Palace by a 3-0 scoreline in the Premier League to climb up to second place in the table and move to within two points of leaders Arsenal. Here, Sports Mole rounds up Man City’s latest injury and suspension news ahead of the contest against the Hammers. Status: Major doubt Type of injury: Hamstring Possible return date: Unknown Oscar Bobb was forced off with an apparent hamstring injury in the EFL Cup win over Brentford and it remains to be seen whether he will be facing a period on the sidelines. Status: Out Type of injury: Leg Possible return date: January 1 (vs. Sunderland) Jeremy Doku has missed Man City’s last two games with a leg injury and Guardiola expects the winger to be sidelined for the next two', "Liverpool injury update: Alexander Isak, Alisson Becker and Jeremie Frimpong latest news and return dates Sport | Football Liverpool returned to winning ways with a 2-0 victory over Aston Villa to end their four-game losing streak in the Premier League. Pressure was piling on Arne Slot after the Reds slumped to ninth in the table, but even without a number of key players, the champions were able to sweep Villa aside to move back up to third. They welcome Real Madrid - Trent Alexander-Arnold, Xabi Alonso, et al - to Anfield in midweek, before they take on Manchester City in a mouth-watering encounter at the weekend. But will they have any of their star names back from injury? Ahead of a big few weeks, here’s all the latest Liverpool injury news and return dates... Alexander Isak has endured a tough start to life on Merseyside. Subbed at half-time in Liverpool’s 5-1 win over Frankfurt in the Champions League, Slot later revealed the £125million summer signing had suffered a minor groin injury. “Alexander had to go off at half-time because he felt his groin a little bit. That's a pity,” said Slot. “I've said many times it is such a difficult balance to find with a player that missed three months.” Isak was not in the squad in the 3-2 loss at Brentford, and he also missed out on the Caraba", "Liverpool injury update: Alexander Isak, Alisson Becker and Jeremie Frimpong latest news and return dates Liverpool returned to winning ways with a 2-0 victory over Aston Villa to end their four-game losing streak in the Premier League. Name: 2_passed away_linda_doris_grandmother
Representative_Docs: ['Mary Kuljis Fiamengo Birth: 1934 Death: 2025 Mary Fiamengo passed away peacefully on November 6, 2025. Mary was born on September 20, 1934 in Komiza, Croatia. She was preceded in death by her beloved husband, Pete J. Fiamengo. Mary was a devoted wife, mother, sister, grandmother, great grandmother, aunt and dear friend. She is survived by her daughters, Deborah Dwight and Jennifer (Tim) Lovoy; her grandchildren, Peter Dwight (Cassandra), John Lovoy (Sloane), Ellen (Justin) Ralph and Lauren Dwight (Tim); her great grandchildren, Jordana Koonce, Hank Ralph and Margot Dwight and her sister, Betty Galosic. She was a long standing member of Mary Star of the Sea Church Velike Gospe Society, San Pedro Peninsula Cancer Guild and San Pedro Peninsula Hospital Bookworm Guild. Mary was a woman of grace, elegance and kindness; always extending a gentle word, a thoughtful gesture or a warm embrace. A memorial mass will be held at 10:30 am on Monday, November 17, 2025 at Mary Star of the Sea Catholic Church. In lieu of flowers, Mary requested that a donation in her memory be made to the San Pedro Peninsula Cancer Guild or to Mary Star of the Sea High School. Send Flowers Plant a Tree Send a Meal', 'Jane Marie Solaro Birth: 1934 Death: 2026 Jane passed away peacefully on January 4, 2026 at the age of 91. Jane was born in Pacific Grove, CA on December 14, 1934 and lived in San Pedro her entire life. She is preceded in death by her loving husband John Solaro and her parents Matthew Bodul & Ann (Berkovich) Bodul. Jane enjoyed the last four years at the Canterbury in Rancho Palos Verdes with her loving friends and caretakers. She is survived by her loving children, Karen Solaro Durber (Martin), Denise Solaro and John Solaro (Lynn). She was a loving grandmother to Anthony Durber (Christina), Michelle Durber Dilly (Ian), Mark Durber, Nikki Solaro, Ali Solaro Sandoval (Dustin), Jack Solaro and Max Solaro and her great grandchildren Brooklynn and Logan Dilly She is also survived by her siblings Bob Bodul (Marylou), Sharon Bodul Werth (Fred) and Matthew Bodul (Angie) as well as several nieces and nephews. She will be deeply missed and remembered by all. Graveside burial at Green Hills Cemetery on Friday, January 23, 2026 @ 2 pm. Celebration of Life to follow. Honor with Flowers In memory of Jane Marie Plant a Living Memorial In memory of Jane Marie', 'Linda Sue (Jordan) Schlosser Birth: 1950 Death: 2025 Linda Sue (Jordan) Schlosser March 16, 1950-December 15, 2025 passed away peacefully surrounded by her family at her home in San Pancho, Mexico. Survived by her husband of 41 years, Phil Schlosser, her mother Gerry, daughter Kelly Noel, son Kevin Noel and wife Jennifer and her grandchildren Trevor and Lena. She was preceded in death by her father, Wayne Henry Jordan and brother Michael Wayne Jordan. Linda was born and raised in San Rafael, CA and graduated from Terra Linda High School. Linda loved sunshine, warm weather and travel. She was an outstanding cook and loved to entertain. Her great adventure was moving to Mexico, building a wonderful home filled with warmth, family, friends and great meals. She loved traveling throughout Mexico and Italy. Her family requests that gifts in lieu of flowers be made in Linda’s name to St. Jude Children’s Research Hospital. Forever deep in my soul, The memory of your love Fills my heart And I am never alone. Honor with Flowers In memory of Linda Sue Plant a Living Memorial In memory of Linda Sue']

Name: 4_passed away_anthony_jim_lived
Representative_Docs: ['Anthony Duarte Birth: 1980 Death: 2025 Anthony Duarte, of La Puente, California, passed away on Wednesday, October 29, 2025, surrounded by love and family. Born on June 9, 1980, in West Covina, California, to Doreen Cardenas and Gildardo Duarte, Anthony was raised with deep love and guidance, and shared a profoundly special bond with his father Robert Cardenas, who helped shape the man he became. Anthony lived a life marked by warmth, creativity, and generosity. His kindness was as constant as his humor, and he approached every day with an open heart and an unwavering sense of purpose. Anthony’s passions reflected his deep belief in connection — through both community and service. He had a profound dedication to civic life, demonstrated by his years of service on the Hacienda La Puente Unified School Board, his time working for a member of Congress, and his contributions to the California Democratic Party. Through these roles, he championed education, representation, and opportunity for others, always guided by empathy and integrity. Anthony cared deeply for his friends and made a lasting impact on everyone lucky enough to know him. Name: 6_family_family members_gifts_friendships
Representative_Docs: ['Asking Eric: Family, friends don’t seem to appreciate time I put into tedious Christmas cookie tradition Dear Eric: My late mother made a special Christmas cookie that required tedious decorating, considering each person’s personal preference for toppings, and giving them as gifts to family and friends. They always had a place of honor on our family Christmas table. When she was no longer able, she gave me the cookie cutter, and my daughters and I took over the family tradition. It takes the three of us several days to make, bake, decorate, package and mail cookies to family members around the country. We enjoy doing it and feel like we’re continuing the fun and festive tradition. However, in recent years, not all of the recipients acknowledge receipt. At some point in their lives, they have all helped Grandma decorate, so they know the time involved. Because of past delivery issues, I track the boxes and inquire if I haven’t heard from them in a couple of days. Last year, one response was “yes we got them.” This feels unappreciative! In the past most would text a picture of their children with the cookies, which we really enjoyed. We rarely see any of them anymore, as they are busy raising their families and don’t live nearby. This seems like a way for us to continue somewhat of a co', 'Asking Eric: Can mom work through tension in son’s relationship with dad so they can celebrate special birthday? Dear Eric: My son is turning 40 on December 22. My husband and I are at a quandary as to how to celebrate him. There have been issues between my husband and him over things from his childhood. We did a special trip for his older brother when he turned 40 and would like to do something special for this son’s 40th as well. Our daughter-in-law has made special plans for him and we are not included. I understand that, but I need some ideas as to how to celebrate this extra special year without rocking the boat. I love my son with all my heart, as I do all my children, and want his 40th birthday to be memorable in a positive way. Any suggestions? – Mom Who Wants to Celebrate Dear Mom: The issues your son and your husband are having may be an obstacle, but they aren’t insurmountable. And the thing that can get them on the path to resolution is the same thing that can get you answers about how to celebrate your son: open communication. It’s not within your purview to try to fix things between them, but you have your own relationship with your son and so I’d encourage you to reach out and tell him what you told me: you want to celebrate him, you know things are difficult, and you’re lookin', 'Asking Eric: After 6 years sober, man is frustrated by his family’s hold on their hurt and distrust Dear Eric: I’ve just turned 40 this past year. The last 15 years I was in a horrible drug addiction. I lied and hurt and did terrible things to a lot of people, especially my family. About eight years ago they officially disowned me. Understandable. I’ve cleaned up and got my act together six years ago. At first, I tried to force my way back into their lives, which all refuted. I lashed out, said horrible things and stopped trying to be in their lives. My mom will stop by on my birthday for 10 minutes or so and drop a card off at Christmas. As for my two older brothers and my father, it’s radio silence. I guess what I’m asking is, what do I do to fix this and fast, as I said I’ve turned 40 this year, my parents are both 70. Time is running out, and I couldn’t imagine living my life without some kind of acceptance from my father. Or knowing he did or does love me. My heart breaks at the thought, but this is a real pickle. How can I fix a problem when the ones I need to fix it with won’t talk to me? Do I just keep ignoring their existence and put on this façade that I don’t care to my wife and 4-year-old son? What picture am I painting to my son, as he’s been guilty by association yo']

Name: 7_fabric_covers_luxurious_garment
Representative_Docs: ["'Luxury' quilted bedspread reduced from £65 'makes my bedroom look expensive' The bedspread has been reduced in price(Image: Dusk ) Finding the perfect finishing touch for the bedroom can be challenging, but sometimes a bedspread is all that's needed to add style and additional warmth. Over at Dusk, there is one bedspread which has been reduced in price and it comes in two colours. The Rome Velvet Greek Matelasse Bedspread is currently available from Dusk for £52, reduced from £65, saving shoppers £13. It also comes in the colour deep pink, but this is for the full price of £65. Dusk says the Rome Velvet Greek Matelasse Bedspread has been designed to add a 'luxurious and inviting finish' to the bedroom, and it features a Greek-inspired matelassé pattern. The velvet-feel fabric creates a 'soft touch', while the intricate quilting enhances its aesthetic, the site says. Shoppers have left rave reviews online The Rome Velvet Greek Matelasse Bedspread, which measures 2.5m x 2.6m, can be styled alone or layered with matching cushion covers. Plus, it is easy to care for, as it can be machine washed at 30 degrees and tumble dried on a low setting - but it is worth noting that it should not be ironed. Name: 9_history_today day_pope_war
Representative_Docs: ['Today in History: April 2, Pope John Paul II dies at 84 Today is Thursday, April 2, the 92nd day of 2026. There are 273 days left in the year. Today in history: On April 2, 2005, John Paul II, the Polish pope born Karol Józef Wojtyła, died in his Vatican apartment at age 84. The first non-Italian pope in over 450 years, John Paul II became one of the most influential leaders of the late 20th and early 21st centuries while playing a crucial role in the fall of communism in Europe and the former Soviet Union. Also on this date: In 1792, Congress passed the Coinage Act, which authorized establishment of the U.S. Mint. In 1865, Confederate President Jefferson Davis and most of his Cabinet fled the Confederate capital of Richmond, Virginia, after Union troops broke through Confederate lines in the Third Battle of Petersburg. In 1917, President Woodrow Wilson asked Congress to declare war against Germany, saying, “The world must be made safe for democracy.” (Congress declared war four days later.) (AP) — “Rental Family” is Brendan Fraser’s first leading role since winning the best actor Oscar for “The Whale.” In it, the 56-year-old plays a struggling actor living in Japan who, out of desperation and curiosity, takes a job with an agency that hires out actors to fulfill roles in real people’s lives. In an interview, Fraser discusses his life since the Oscar win, his eye-opening experiences in Japan and what returning to the “Mummy” series means to him. His time in Japan included a visit to a hedgehog cafe. “Rental Family” opens Friday in theaters. Edgerton stars alongside Felicity Jones and William H. Macy in the meditative film. Name: 11_travel_tourism_world cup_events
Representative_Docs: ['Sporting And Cultural Events Boost Travel Intentions For 2026 Photo Credit: Deposit Photos Thirty-four percent of American travelers say they are likely to take an overnight trip to attend a sporting event in 2026. Twenty percent plan to attend a FIFA World Cup match, and 52% intend to travel overnight for cultural events such as concerts and festivals. Meanwhile, 47% are interested in touring Route 66 during its 100th anniversary year. These figures highlight a shift for the coming year where travel decisions are increasingly driven by events rather than relaxation. In 2026, Americans are choosing what to attend, not just where to go. The latest Longwoods International study shows that milestone celebrations and global competitions are now major motivators for travel. The calendar is driving demand. “Special events such as the FIFA World Cup, America 250 and the 100th anniversary of Route 66 will all motivate U.S. travel this year,” said Amir Eylon, President and CEO of Longwoods International. The industry has emphasized experience-based travel, but 2026 denotes a more specific trend. Events offer deadlines, shared participation, and reasons to plan trips in advance. The FIFA World Cup brings global visibility and international visitors. Name: 12_museum_egypt_egyptian_world largest
Representative_Docs: ["Egypt unveils the world's largest museum dedicated to a single civilization CAIRO (AP) — Egypt is inaugurating on Saturday the Grand Egyptian Museum, two decades in the making, with the aim of drawing more visitors to revitalize the country's tourist industry. Located just outside Cairo on the Giza Plateau which also includes the three pyramids and the Sphinx, the museum will be the world’s largest dedicated to a single civilization. It will display over 50,000 artifacts detailing life in ancient Egypt. World leaders, including monarchs, heads of states and governments, are set to attend the grand opening ceremony, according to the Egyptian presidency which touted the museum as “an exceptional event in the history of human culture and civilization.” The museum is one of the megaprojects championed by President Abdel-Fattah el-Sissi since he took office in 2014. He has embarked on massive investments in infrastructure with the aim of reviving an economy weakened by decades of stagnation and battered by the unrest that followed the 2011 Arab Spring uprising. Preparations for the grand reveal have been shrouded in secrecy. The museum, which was opened for limited visits in recent years, has been closed for the past two weeks. Name: 15_welfare_wages_rising costs_social security
Representative_Docs: ['How much will DWP benefits and pensions rise following the budget? Benefits Budget 2025 Child Benefit Department for Work and Pensions DWP Pensions PIP Rachel Reeves Universal Credit National government Personal Finance DWP benefits including Universal Credit, Child Benefit, PIP, Carer\'s Allowance, and the State Pension are to increase in April 2026 after Rachel Reeves announced higher payments in her budget speech today. Anyone who claims DWP benefits should be in line for an increase in the next financial year, as the chancellor laid out plans to help pensioners, disabled people, parents, low earners, carers and unemployed people keep up with rising costs. Addressing MPs, Ms Reeves said: "The broken welfare system that we inherited wrote off millions of people as too sick to work and we will reform that system so that it is a system that does not count the cost of failure, but one that protects people who cannot work and empowers those who can. "We have brought back face-to-face assessments for disability benefits. Name: 16_garden_village_scenic_accommodation
Representative_Docs: ["Alan Titchmarsh fans in tears as couple with '150 kids' have garden ruined in real time ITV fans couldn’t believe it when Alan Titchmarsh “ruined” a couple’s garden on Love Your Garden – especially after elderly couple Rob and Margaret Isdale had fostered more than 150 children over the past 45 years. Their “lovely” garden in Grantham featured on the show as Alan and his co-stars Katie Rushworth, David Domoney and Frances Tophill tried to transform it into something inspired by The Secret Garden. Initially, the space featured a large lawn area with a shed and concrete paving stones, surrounded by lush trees and greenery. Afterwards, it was transformed into a floral haven complete with a faux-ruined stone wall, stone paving stones leading in paths around the flowers, and wooden trellises around the side of the house. The Dreamcatcher Wagon is nestled in the village of Craswall, close to the border with Wales and enveloped by the dramatic landscape of the Black Mountains. The setting provides an ideal mix of tranquillity and chances to explore. Village shops with local produce and a traditional pub are within easy walking distance. Close to Hay‐on‐Wye, on the fringes of the Brecon Beacons National Park, the accommodation boasts stunning vistas across the surrounding countryside, allowing guests to feel truly connected with nature. It also has a private hot tub among its creature comforts. March availability for a seven-night break is currently priced at £488, down from £537 and there is still some availability through spring and summer. Perfect for two guests, the secluded off-grid retreat sits within a tranquil 17-acre farm that's home to horses, donkeys and chickens. The wagon boasts an attractive arched roof and carefully crafted interiors, delivering a warm yet elegant ambience. Representative_Docs: ["California lawmakers push back against offshore oil drilling (The Center Square) – Weeks after California Lt. Gov. Eleni Kounalakis spoke out against federal efforts to expand offshore oil drilling, state lawmakers told The Center Square that increased drilling is deeply unpopular among coastal residents. Opponents warn against the environmental costs. But supporters say technology has made drilling safer. Representative_Docs: ['Luckfox 16-inch 4K 120Hz portable touch monitor offers 100% sRGB coverage The segment of portable monitors continues to grow, with many models available on Amazon at relatively affordable prices, typically between $100 and $150. These screens can be useful for productivity on business trips or while traveling. But portable monitors that combine a high resolution with a fast refresh rate remain uncommon in the mid-range segment. Luckfox wants to address this gap with its new 16" 4K Touch Monitor. The 16-inch display features a 3840 × 2400 (WQUXGA) IPS panel with 350 nits brightness and a 1200:1 contrast ratio. It uses optical bonding technology with 6H-hardness toughened glass and a matte finish to minimize ambient reflections. The 16:10 aspect ratio provides more vertical workspace than the standard 16:9 format. It also supports a 120Hz refresh rate, which is an upgrade over the common 60Hz panels found in many portable monitors, such as the Auzai ME16Z01 or the Arzopa A1M; response times aren\'t disclosed, though. As for connectivity, the product integrates two USB Type-C ports and a Mini HDMI input. Both USB-C ports support audio, video, and power delivery, meaning that only a single USB-C cable is required when connected to a compatible smartphone, tab', 'Samsung OLED 48S95H: New 48-inch and 165 Hz OLED TV revealed ahead of schedule with HDR10 Plus Advanced support Samsung has already teased plenty of devices on the eve of CES 2026. However, the trade show has decided to lift the lid on one of Samsung\'s forthcoming OLED Smart TVs. For context, Samsung listed numerous unreleased Smart TVs on its Parts Database over a month ago. Assuming a 4K resolution from the S95F series and the usage of HDMI 2.1 ports, a 165 Hz refresh rate would require the usage of Display Stream Compression (DSC) to output at its peak resolution and refresh rate simultaneously. Moreover, the new 48-inch Smart TV will support Samsung\'s new HDR10 Plus Advanced format. Pricing and availability are unknown for now; the full description provided by Samsung and CES is as follows: The Samsung OLED (S95H) is the world\'s most advanced 48" OLED TV for high-end gaming. Name: 21_immigration_britain_british government_immigrants
Representative_Docs: ['‘UK public have tougher stance on immigration and diversity than people in US’ People in the UK are less likely than their US counterparts to feel diversity strengthens society and more likely to have tougher attitudes on immigration, according to research. The findings are said to show that a common narrative about Britain being more socially liberal than America does not ring true across all issues. The National Centre for Social Research also found Reform UK voters in Britain were more likely than supporters of President Donald Trump in the US to say immigrants living in their country without permission should not be allowed to stay. While seven in 10 supporters of Mr Trump felt this way, the figure was almost eight in 10 (79%) among Reform followers. The researchers said they wanted to “better understand how public attitudes in the UK compare with those in the US, and the extent of polarisation in the two countries”. Their survey found 42% of people in the UK think there should be legal efforts to deport immigrants living here without permission, compared with just a third (33%) in the US. While almost two-thirds (64%) of respondents in the US felt diversity strengthens society, only around half (49%) of the UK public agreed. Representative_Docs: ['\'Super flu\' is posing threat to NHS unlike any since Covid pandemic, warns Wes Streeting News | Politics Get our award-winning daily news email featuring exclusive stories, opinion and expert analysis I would like to be emailed about offers, event and updates from Evening Standard. Read our privacy notice. Flu is tearing through London’s hospitals, Wes Streeting is warning as he urged doctors to call off their five-day strike to protect the NHS in the run-up to Christmas. The Health Secretary issued his plea as latest figures showed flu hospitalisation cases in the capital had jumped 47% in just a week. Mr Streeting told The Standard: "There is a tidal wave of flu tearing through our London hospitals with hundreds of beds in the capital filled with flu patients – the highest recorded at this time of year. “I have seen the damage flu is doing in my local hospitals and it isn’t acceptable for patients.” The MP for Ilford North added: “With hospital beds, ambulances and A&Es in London filling up with patients, the whole NHS team is working around the clock to keep our families safe. “I’m urging resident doctors to call off strike action, this Christmas, which would come at the worst possible time.” The Health Secretary stressed: “The offer I have made to the BMA would hel', 'UK households with washing machine in kitchen risk £59 charge UK households have been warned over how much it costs to use a washing machine for an hour. In The Wash looked at 20 of the most popular washing machines in the UK to calculate the average amount of electricity and water used per cycle. The average washing machine cycle uses 0.46 kWh of electricity per cycle, with the average electricy costing 26.35 pence per kWh for the Energy Price Cap (EPC) period from 1 October to 31 December 2025. This means that the average washing machine costs 12.1 pence per cycle. It means if you use your washing machine 220 times a year, it would cost you £26.67 in electricity costs. READ MORE 1.6 million drivers handed £160 fine \'after wanting to follow rules\' The average model uses 47.65 litres of water per cycle, at a cost of water per litre of around 0.3 pence per litre. This means that the average washing machine uses 14.3 pence water per cycle. If you use your washing machine 220 times a year, it would cost you £31.45 in water costs. Adding together electricity and water costs, the average washing machine therefore costs 26.4 pence per cycle to run - or £59 a year. Which? have shared how people may be able to operate their tumble dryers for less. According to the consumer champion platform, cheaper off-peak electricity tends to be available after 10pm and before 8am. Will Owen says households should put their clothes on a spin cycle if there clothes are still very wet when they take them out of the washing machine. He said using the spin cycle will remove excess water, “meaning your clothes will dry faster in the tumble dryer or on an airer”. Name: 23_racing_raceway_fourth place_qualifying
Representative_Docs: ["Denny Hamlin qualifies first at Martinsville, moves into tie for 10th in NASCAR poles MARTINSVILLE, Va. — Denny Hamlin earned his fifth pole position at Martinsville Speedway and 49th of his Cup Series career, tying him with NASCAR Hall of Famer Bobby Isaac for 10th all time. The Joe Gibbs Racing star turned a 98.241-mph lap Saturday in the No. 11 Toyota to beat William Byron, whose No. 24 Chevrolet qualified second at 97.957 mph for Sunday’s 400-lap race. With his win two weeks ago at Las Vegas Motor Speedway, Hamlin, 45, broke a tie with Kevin Harvick for 10th on the all-time win list in NASCAR's premier series. He is two poles away from tying the career total of Ryan Newman, one of the greatest qualifiers in NASCAR history. “It’s pretty awesome,” said Hamlin, who grew up a few hours east of Martinsville in the Richmond, Virginia, suburb of Chesterfield. “Really with age, the hardest part is still having the fast time. It’s one thing to be able to manage races and use your experience to your advantage. But usually the first thing to go is your all-out speed, and we’re still knocking off poles, which is really good. I was around when Newman was just unstoppable in qualifying. So damn, I didn’t know he had that many.” Hamlin has six wins at Martinsville, and his vic", "Denny Hamlin qualifies first at Martinsville, moves into tie for 10th in NASCAR poles MARTINSVILLE, Va. — Denny Hamlin earned his fifth pole position at Martinsville Speedway and 49th of his Cup Series career, tying him with NASCAR Hall of Famer Bobby Isaac for 10th all time. Name: 25_florence_meeting_italy_economy
Representative_Docs: ['Florence Press Conference Confirms ECB Remains Comfortable in Its Good Place Even a change of scenery has done nothing to change the European Central Bank’s current wait-and-see stance. Florence, where the ECB met today for its only annual meeting outside of Frankfurt, is apparently also this ’good place’, the central bank’s well-known pseudonym for comfortably keeping interest rates on hold. ECB President Christine Lagarde even called it a ‘magnificent’ place – Florence, that is, not rates. The comments at the press conference confirmed once again that the ECB has no intention to leave its ‘good place’ any time soon. We had feared a rather uneventful press conference. What should we say? We hate to be right… Anyhow, here are a few interesting remarks made by Lagarde: On the ECB’s risk assessment, the introductory statement had the most remarkable change compared with September. Last month, the ECB had still talked about risks to the growth outlook that had become “more balanced”; today, it refrained from qualifying any balance of risk. Instead, the central bank had a somewhat more extensive list of upside and downside risks to the growth outlook. Lagarde even said that some of the downside risks had abated.
